textPlain: Title should include (2015). It's been posted before and for good reasons, it's a piece which lists very good examples of normalization of deviance in organizations.Personally it happened to myself as well. I regularly rent a Tesla and once, I took a date on a trip and I drove us through the city, her riding shotgun. She said: "Look at the orange line on the screen. You're driving too close to the parked cars on our right".I answered, "it always does that, the proximity sensor on these Teslas is way too nervous". She looked out of the window and said: "no, you are actually way too close to the parked cars!"I had totally normalized the proximity alarm.

Edit: seems related to this ongoing thread: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=45149336Related.
